notes:
có thể means can / be able to. It also means maybe / possibly when talking about uncertainty.

It goes before a verb to show ability or permission.  

It can also start a sentence to mean “maybe,” often with là for guessing.  

Close to có khả năng (more formal, more about probability). Opposite idea: không thể = cannot / impossible.
sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + có thể (can) + mở (open) + cửa sổ (window)
Chị (she) + có thể (can) + uống (drink) + nước lạnh (cold water)
Anh (he) + có thể (can) + sửa (fix) + máy tính (computer)
notes_md:
**có thể** means **can / be able to**. It also means **maybe / possibly** when talking about uncertainty.

It goes before a verb to show ability or permission.  

It can also start a sentence to mean “maybe,” often with **là** for guessing.  

Close to **có khả năng** (more formal, more about probability). Opposite idea: **không thể** = cannot / impossible.
exampleSentences: Em có thể mở cửa sổ.
Chị có thể uống nước lạnh.
Anh có thể sửa máy tính.
